div1.entryFree: Domestic, adj., pertaining to a person's own country or nation, intestine, not foreign: d. broils, R3 II, 4, 60. d. awe, night-rest and neighbour-hood, Tim. IV, 1, 17 (i. e. all reverence for the duties of society). d. fury, Caes. III, 1, 263. malice d. Mcb. III, 2, 25. broils, Lr. V, 1, 30. quarrel, Oth. II, 3, 215. equality of two d. powers, Ant. I, 3, 47. Caesar, that hath more kings his servants than thyself d. officers, Cymb. III, 1, 65 (== officers in thy own country).
